posts.name: David Bueker
posts.cooked:
• 2012 Domaine Georges Mugneret-Gibourg Vosne-Romanée - France, Burgundy, Côte de Nuits, Vosne-Romanée (5/10/2025)
    Tasted over the course of two evenings, this was a good, though not at all great M-G VR. No surprise given the vintage, but I was hoping for a little more depth. Instead it was more of a pretty wine which is its own virtue. Very floral, with moderate red fruits, and some sous bois elements that appeared with air. Balance is lovely, with more of an acid than tannic core, so it was not at all drying. I actually preferred sipping this on its own, as flavorful food drowned it out to a great degree. The last glass, after dinner, was a charming drink.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: This was so good from barrel, but hasn’t quite delivered on all its seeming potential… I think the vintage is better than you allow, however, in the Côte de Nuits especially.

posts.name: David Bueker
posts.cooked: You would know better. 2011-2013 is an almost complete hole in my Burgundy buying.

posts.name: Erlend @ntonsen
posts.cooked: Any thoughts on the reasons why some wines fail to deliver on barrel promise?

posts.name: Mich@el Ch@ng
posts.cooked: Yeah we had some 12s last night that were pretty fantastic, although I do think at the grand cru level some wines probably could use a bit more cellar time.

posts.name: Brady Daniels
posts.cooked:
David_Bu3ker:

2011-2013 is an almost complete hole in my Burgundy buying.

I’m struggling to think of a better three vintage spread from this millennium to miss. 2012 is good, but 2011 poor, and 2013 hit-or-miss for me.

Maybe 04-06, if you don’t believe 05 will ever come around, or 18-20 if you don’t appreciate modern ripe vintages.

posts.name: Mich@el Ch@ng
posts.cooked:
Brady_Daniels:
David_Bu3ker:

2011-2013 is an almost complete hole in my Burgundy buying.

I’m struggling to think of a better three vintage spread from this millennium to miss. 2012 is good, but 2011 poor, and 2013 hit-or-miss for me.

Maybe 04-06, if you don’t believe 05 will ever come around, or 18-20 if you don’t appreciate modern ripe vintages.

03-05 is probably rhe worst. 18-20 for me is likely close to the best 3 year period in the this century, although probably I’d take 19-21 as the actual best.

Other good ones would be 15-17, 08-10, 14-16, 00-02.

13 is definitely hit or miss but there are some excellent wines from 13, especially from Rousseau.

posts.name: Yule Kim
posts.cooked:
MChang:

Yeah we had some 12s last night that were pretty fantastic, although I do think at the grand cru level some wines probably could use a bit more cellar time.

Have you had any 2012 Lignier MSD 1er Cru VV. I was wondering whether it’s approachable at this point?

posts.name: Howard Cooper
posts.cooked:
Brady_Daniels:
David_Bu3ker:

2011-2013 is an almost complete hole in my Burgundy buying.

I’m struggling to think of a better three vintage spread from this millennium to miss. 2012 is good, but 2011 poor, and 2013 hit-or-miss for me.

Maybe 04-06, if you don’t believe 05 will ever come around, or 18-20 if you don’t appreciate modern ripe vintages.

I have had some good 12s and 13s, although neither is a great vintage.

I have been finding some 2005s this past year that have given me hope that the vintage is finally starting to come around. I really liked a Bouchard Beaune Therons and a Rossignol-Trapet GC that I had over the last six months for example.

posts.name: Mich@el Ch@ng
posts.cooked: Nope, just the CdlR, but my guess is that the msd 1er vv should be reasonably accessible based on how the CdlR did with a bit of air time.

posts.name: Mich@el Ch@ng
posts.cooked: I’ve found wines from the cdb to be more accessible from 05 than cdn at this point.
